content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach is reaching for AoA-style moves — asking about cost, redirecting from content to felt experience, noticing the speed of the move from feeling to fixing — rather than offering pragmatic advice or selling the methodology. That's a real strength and avoids the major traps. However, the timing and sequencing are mixed. The client opens with 'I don't have time for this,' which calls for meeting the resistance directly (meet-resistance-with-okay, refusing the dichotomy, a concrete frame for 'the work is the how'). Instead, the coach skips that regulation/framing step and goes straight to inquiry-into-feeling by turn 8-12. The client is still in rush/defended mode — the move 'what's underneath not great' arrives before the dichotomy has been addressed, so it lands as the coach trying to take the client somewhere the client hasn't agreed to go. By turn 11 the client pushes back ('What are you actually asking me?'), and instead of pivoting, the coach doubles down with the same move in turn 12 ('what do you feel when...'). That's a repeat rather than a read-and-adjust. So: right family of moves, wrong sequence, and a double-down when the move missed.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 8, 10, 11, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opens with a question about cost — reasonable, but bypasses the explicit resistance ('I need it to be efficient') rather than meeting it."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Good move — turns attention to what brought the client here, gently surfacing the contradiction without arguing the dichotomy."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Follows the content thread; neither advances nor harms the work."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the speed of 'she's not wrong' and invites felt experience — a proper AoA move, but arriving before the rush has been met."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Names the pattern (fast move from feeling to fixing) — good noticing, but the client isn't regulated enough to receive it."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client signals the move didn't land ('What are you actually asking me?') — a clear cue to pivot."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach doubles down with the same feeling-inquiry move rather than pivoting to meet the resistance or offer a concrete frame for 'the work is the how.'"}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "the-rush-signature", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "Coach avoided the big traps (didn't sell, didn't advise, didn't evangelize) but missed the central move the scenario calls for: meeting the resistance and refusing the dichotomy with a concrete frame. Sequence error: jumped to feeling-inquiry before earning it, then repeated the move when it missed."
}
